{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Female School Receptionist",
  "description": "MBIS International is a growing nursery and primary school delivering the British Curriculum. The school is committed to maintaining high standards of teaching, learning and pupil achievement.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Female School Receptionist

Job Location:&nbsp;Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	Our front office plays an important role in shaping the daily experience of pupils, parents and visitors. We are therefore seeking a&nbsp;highly organised, professional and dependable Female School Receptionist&nbsp;who can manage the school&rsquo;s reception with efficiency, warmth and attention to detail.
	The successful candidate will serve as the&nbsp;first point of contact for the school community, ensuring that every interaction reflects the school&rsquo;s high standards of professionalism, courtesy and care.
	This role requires an individual who is attentive, proactive and capable of maintaining a calm and welcoming presence in a busy school environment.


Key Responsibilities
Reception and Front Office Management:


	Welcome pupils, parents, staff and visitors in a warm, courteous and professional manner.
	Serve as the first point of contact for all front desk enquiries and ensure they are handled efficiently.
	Ensure all visitors sign in and out in line with the school&rsquo;s safeguarding procedures.
	Direct enquiries appropriately and provide clear information regarding school procedures where required.
	Maintain a calm, organised and welcoming reception environment throughout the school day.


Telephone and Enquiry Management:


	Answer incoming calls promptly and professionally, ensuring all enquiries are handled courteously and accurately.
	Take clear messages where necessary and ensure they are delivered to the appropriate staff members promptly.
	Maintain a polite, calm and professional telephone manner at all times.


Attendance and Record Keeping:


	Accurately record pupil attendance each day.
	Follow up on unexplained absences in accordance with school procedures.
	Maintain clear records of late arrivals and early departures.
	Ensure all attendance records and relevant databases remain accurate and up to date.


Admissions Enquiries and Prospective Parent Engagement


	Serve as the first point of contact for prospective parents making enquiries about the school.
	Confidently provide clear and accurate information to prospective parents regarding the school&rsquo;s programmes, admissions procedures and general expectations of the school.
	Record and maintain accurate details of all admission enquiries received via phone, email or walk-in visits.
	Schedule school visits or appointments for prospective parents when required.
	Ensure all admission enquiries are properly logged, communicated to leadership and followed-up.


Administrative Support:


	Provide administrative support including photocopying, printing and laminating as required.
	Maintain clear and legible handwritten records where necessary.
	Take meeting notes and produce well-structured minutes.


Reception Environment:


	Ensure reception and office notice boards are kept up to date and professionally presented.
	Maintain a tidy, organised and welcoming front office environment at all times.


Parent and Pupil Support:


	Respond courteously to parent enquiries regarding school procedures and schedules.
	Provide support to pupils with minor concerns such as lost items.


Front of House Expectations


	As the first point of contact for the school, the Receptionist is expected to consistently demonstrate a high standard of professionalism.
	Maintain a&nbsp;neat, modest and professional appearance&nbsp;suitable for a school environment.
	Interact with parents confidently, courteously and professionally, ensuring that all enquiries are handled clearly and reflect the school&rsquo;s high standards of service.
	Demonstrate&nbsp;discretion and confidentiality&nbsp;when handling school information
	Demonstrate confidence, initiative and sound judgement when handling responsibilities and supporting the smooth running of the school office.
	Maintain a calm, solution-focused approach when responding to enquiries or addressing concerns.
	Operate in full alignment with school policies and procedures, ensuring that all front office activities are carried out in line with safeguarding standards and established school protocols


Ideal Candidate:


	Applicants must reside in&nbsp;Ibadan&nbsp;and be able to commute easily toJericho, Ibadan.
	The ideal candidate will be a well-presented, composed and highly organised individual who understands the importance of first impressions in a school environment.


Requirements
Education:


	A Degree in Administration, Management or a related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of three years&rsquo; continuous experience working in a well-organised school with clear administrative systems and procedures or a structured front desk/reception role within a professional service environment such as education, hospitality, healthcare or corporate office administration.
	Experience in structured, client-facing environments requiring professionalism, discretion and strong communication skills will be highly advantageous.


Personal Attributes
The ideal candidate should demonstrate:


	Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage confidently and professionally with parents and visitors.
	Strong interpersonal presence with warmth, courtesy and professional boundaries.
	High level of organisation and attention to detail.
	Calm, composed and solution-focused approach under pressure.
	Initiative and reliability in managing responsibilities without constant supervision.
	Professional discretion and confidentiality at all times.
	Strong digital competence, including Microsoft Office and basic administrative systems.
	Must demonstrate clear and legible handwriting suitable&nbsp;for maintaining professional school records and documentation.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to:&nbsp;eyfsrecruitment@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;Application &ndash; Female School Receptionist &ndash; Jericho&quot;&nbsp;as the subject of the mail.

Send the following documents to:


	Your CV
	A&nbsp;one-page handwritten statementexplaining why you believe you are suitable for the role (scanned and attached)
	A&nbsp;full-length photograph.


Additional Application Instructions:


	Applications that do not follow the stated instructions will not be reviewed
	Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the next stag of the selection process.


Note:&nbsp;If you believe you have the professionalism, organisation and commitment required to excel in this role, we would be pleased to hear from you.",

Female School Receptionist Job at MBIS International




        
    

        
        MBIS International is a growing nursery and primary school delivering the British Curriculum. The school is committed to maintaining high standards of teaching, learning and pupil achievement.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Female School Receptionist

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our front office plays an important role in shaping the daily experience of pupils, parents and visitors. We are therefore seeking a highly organised, professional and dependable Female School Receptionist who can manage the school’s reception with efficiency, warmth and attention to detail.
	The successful candidate will serve as the first point of contact for the school community, ensuring that every interaction reflects the school’s high standards of professionalism, courtesy and care.
	This role requires an individual who is attentive, proactive and capable of maintaining a calm and welcoming presence in a busy school environment.


Key Responsibilities
Reception and Front Office Management:


	Welcome pupils, parents, staff and visitors in a warm, courteous and professional manner.
	Serve as the first point of contact for all front desk enquiries and ensure they are handled efficiently.
	Ensure all visitors sign in and out in line with the school’s safeguarding procedures.
	Direct enquiries appropriately and provide clear information regarding school procedures where required.
	Maintain a calm, organised and welcoming reception environment throughout the school day.


Telephone and Enquiry Management:


	Answer incoming calls promptly and professionally, ensuring all enquiries are handled courteously and accurately.
	Take clear messages where necessary and ensure they are delivered to the appropriate staff members promptly.
	Maintain a polite, calm and professional telephone manner at all times.


Attendance and Record Keeping:


	Accurately record pupil attendance each day.
	Follow up on unexplained absences in accordance with school procedures.
	Maintain clear records of late arrivals and early departures.
	Ensure all attendance records and relevant databases remain accurate and up to date.


Admissions Enquiries and Prospective Parent Engagement


	Serve as the first point of contact for prospective parents making enquiries about the school.
	Confidently provide clear and accurate information to prospective parents regarding the school’s programmes, admissions procedures and general expectations of the school.
	Record and maintain accurate details of all admission enquiries received via phone, email or walk-in visits.
	Schedule school visits or appointments for prospective parents when required.
	Ensure all admission enquiries are properly logged, communicated to leadership and followed-up.


Administrative Support:


	Provide administrative support including photocopying, printing and laminating as required.
	Maintain clear and legible handwritten records where necessary.
	Take meeting notes and produce well-structured minutes.


Reception Environment:


	Ensure reception and office notice boards are kept up to date and professionally presented.
	Maintain a tidy, organised and welcoming front office environment at all times.


Parent and Pupil Support:


	Respond courteously to parent enquiries regarding school procedures and schedules.
	Provide support to pupils with minor concerns such as lost items.


Front of House Expectations


	As the first point of contact for the school, the Receptionist is expected to consistently demonstrate a high standard of professionalism.
	Maintain a neat, modest and professional appearance suitable for a school environment.
	Interact with parents confidently, courteously and professionally, ensuring that all enquiries are handled clearly and reflect the school’s high standards of service.
	Demonstrate discretion and confidentiality when handling school information
	Demonstrate confidence, initiative and sound judgement when handling responsibilities and supporting the smooth running of the school office.
	Maintain a calm, solution-focused approach when responding to enquiries or addressing concerns.
	Operate in full alignment with school policies and procedures, ensuring that all front office activities are carried out in line with safeguarding standards and established school protocols


Ideal Candidate:


	Applicants must reside in Ibadan and be able to commute easily toJericho, Ibadan.
	The ideal candidate will be a well-presented, composed and highly organised individual who understands the importance of first impressions in a school environment.


Requirements
Education:


	A Degree in Administration, Management or a related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of three years’ continuous experience working in a well-organised school with clear administrative systems and procedures or a structured front desk/reception role within a professional service environment such as education, hospitality, healthcare or corporate office administration.
	Experience in structured, client-facing environments requiring professionalism, discretion and strong communication skills will be highly advantageous.


Personal Attributes
The ideal candidate should demonstrate:


	Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage confidently and professionally with parents and visitors.
	Strong interpersonal presence with warmth, courtesy and professional boundaries.
	High level of organisation and attention to detail.
	Calm, composed and solution-focused approach under pressure.
	Initiative and reliability in managing responsibilities without constant supervision.
	Professional discretion and confidentiality at all times.
	Strong digital competence, including Microsoft Office and basic administrative systems.
	Must demonstrate clear and legible handwriting suitable for maintaining professional school records and documentation.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: [email protected] using "Application – Female School Receptionist – Jericho" as the subject of the mail.

Send the following documents to:


	Your CV
	A one-page handwritten statementexplaining why you believe you are suitable for the role (scanned and attached)
	A full-length photograph.


Additional Application Instructions:


	Applications that do not follow the stated instructions will not be reviewed
	Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the next stag of the selection process.


Note: If you believe you have the professionalism, organisation and commitment required to excel in this role, we would be pleased to hear from you.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "First Metro Infrastructure Limited -&nbsp;As the preferred concessionaire of the Red Line Project in Lagos State, FMIL is an end-to-end railway value creation company committed to developing world-class railway infrastructure solutions for the people of Lagos. Our unwavering commitment to delivering safe, timely, and cost-effective projects is driven by our passion for driving progress and transforming transportation in the region.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Human Resources Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Key Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end recruitment, including sourcing, screening, and coordinating interviews.
	Develop and maintain strong talent pipelines to meet ongoing hiring needs.
	Support employer branding initiatives to attract quality candidates.
	Maintain accurate employee records and ensure HR documentation compliance.
	Conduct background checks and reference verifications for shortlisted candidates.
	Coordinate onboarding and orientation of new employees.
	Provide general HR support to employees and assist in day-to-day HR operations.


Requirements


	BSc/HND in Human Resources Management or related field
	3 years of relevant HR experience in a structured environment
	Proficiency in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and HR/recruitment tools (ATS is an advantage)
	Strong communication, organization, and attention-to-detail skills
	CIPM certification is an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@first-metro.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as subject of email.",

Human Resources Officer Job at First Metro Infrastructure Limited




        
    

        
        First Metro Infrastructure Limited - As the preferred concessionaire of the Red Line Project in Lagos State, FMIL is an end-to-end railway value creation company committed to developing world-class railway infrastructure solutions for the people of Lagos. Our unwavering commitment to delivering safe, timely, and cost-effective projects is driven by our passion for driving progress and transforming transportation in the region.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage end-to-end recruitment, including sourcing, screening, and coordinating interviews.
	Develop and maintain strong talent pipelines to meet ongoing hiring needs.
	Support employer branding initiatives to attract quality candidates.
	Maintain accurate employee records and ensure HR documentation compliance.
	Conduct background checks and reference verifications for shortlisted candidates.
	Coordinate onboarding and orientation of new employees.
	Provide general HR support to employees and assist in day-to-day HR operations.


Requirements


	BSc/HND in Human Resources Management or related field
	3 years of relevant HR experience in a structured environment
	Proficiency in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and HR/recruitment tools (ATS is an advantage)
	Strong communication, organization, and attention-to-detail skills
	CIPM certification is an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Facility Manager",
  "description": "Ascent Resources,&nbsp;an&nbsp;HR Consulting Company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Anthony Village, Lagos Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	An indigenous group of the Company located in the Anthony Village area of Lagos Mainland urgently seeks to employ a Facility Manager. This is an Immediate resumption role.


Job Summary


	We are seeking ahighly experienced, hands-on, and technically proficient Senior Facility Managerto oversee the complete management, maintenance, and operations of our properties. The ideal candidate must possess deep technical expertise in power infrastructure and building maintenance, combined with strong leadership and stakeholder management skills.
	This is not a desk role; we require a practical, solution-driven professional with proven experience managing complex power systems and building fabric.


Key Responsibilities
Power &amp; Technical Infrastructure Management:


	Design and manage robust power solutions, ensuring near 100% uptime across all our properties.
	Oversee all power systems, includingDiesel Generators, Gas Generators, Solar PV Systems, Inverters, UPS, and EKEDCinterface management.
	Take full ownership ofHVAC/Air Conditioning systems&mdash; maintenance, troubleshooting, preventive servicing, and performance optimization.
	Develop and implement preventive and predictive maintenance schedules for all critical assets.


Property Operations &amp; Maintenance:


	Lead the day-to-day technical operations, maintenance, and appearance of buildings, common areas, and facilities.
	Conduct regular technical inspections and ensure timely repairs of plumbing, electrical, civil works, painting, and structural elements.
	Coordinate major and minor refurbishment works, painting cycles, and facility upgrades.


Vendor &amp; Contractor Management:


	Identify, engage, supervise, and evaluate technical vendors, contractors, cleaners, security, and service providers.
	Negotiate contracts, monitor SLAs, and ensure strict compliance with quality, safety, and performance standards.


Client &amp; Stakeholder Management:


	Serve as the primary point of contact for all facility-related matters.
	Manage user complaints professionally while maintaining excellent relationships.
	Ensure high levels of client satisfaction through proactive communication and timely issue resolution.


Reporting, Compliance &amp; Finance:


	Prepare detailed monthly technical and operational reports on asset performance, maintenance activities, power consumption, and expenses.
	Maintain accurate records of maintenance history, warranties, service contracts, and regulatory compliance.
	Manage facility budgets, control costs, and identify opportunities for operational efficiency.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	10 - 15 years of post-qualification experience in Facility Management, with at least 5 years in a senior technical role.
	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree inElectrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Estate Management, or Facilities Management. (A Master&rsquo;s degree or relevant professional certifications).
	Strong hands-on experiencein Power Management &mdash; Generators (Diesel &amp; Gas), Solar Systems, EKEDC coordination, and Air Conditioning systems ismandatory.
	Proven track record in building maintenance, painting schedules, civil repairs, and facility refurbishment.
	Excellent vendor management, negotiation, and client relationship skills.
	Strong knowledge of Health, Safety, and Environmental regulations.


Key Competencies:


	Exceptional problem-solving and decision-making skills under pressure.
	Strong leadership and team management ability.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Highly organized with strong attention to detail.
	Ability to respond swiftly and effectively to emergencies.


What We Offer


	Competitive remuneration package (?350,000 &ndash; ?450,000 gross per month)
	Comprehensive Health Insurance (HMO)
	Pension Scheme
	Professional development and certification support.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested andsuitably qualified candidatesshould send theirdetailed CV and abrief Cover Letter to:&nbsp;logisticjob18@gmail.com&nbsp;using&nbsp;&quot;Senior Facility Manager &ndash; Anthony, Lagos Mainland&quot; as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only candidates with a minimum of 10 years of relevant experience will be considered.
	Applications without the correct email subject will not be treated.

Facility Manager Job at Ascent Resources




        
    

        
        Ascent Resources, an HR Consulting Company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility Manager

Job Location: Anthony Village, Lagos Mainland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	An indigenous group of the Company located in the Anthony Village area of Lagos Mainland urgently seeks to employ a Facility Manager. This is an Immediate resumption role.


Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking ahighly experienced, hands-on, and technically proficient Senior Facility Managerto oversee the complete management, maintenance, and operations of our properties. The ideal candidate must possess deep technical expertise in power infrastructure and building maintenance, combined with strong leadership and stakeholder management skills.
	This is not a desk role; we require a practical, solution-driven professional with proven experience managing complex power systems and building fabric.


Key Responsibilities
Power & Technical Infrastructure Management:


	Design and manage robust power solutions, ensuring near 100% uptime across all our properties.
	Oversee all power systems, includingDiesel Generators, Gas Generators, Solar PV Systems, Inverters, UPS, and EKEDCinterface management.
	Take full ownership ofHVAC/Air Conditioning systems— maintenance, troubleshooting, preventive servicing, and performance optimization.
	Develop and implement preventive and predictive maintenance schedules for all critical assets.


Property Operations & Maintenance:


	Lead the day-to-day technical operations, maintenance, and appearance of buildings, common areas, and facilities.
	Conduct regular technical inspections and ensure timely repairs of plumbing, electrical, civil works, painting, and structural elements.
	Coordinate major and minor refurbishment works, painting cycles, and facility upgrades.


Vendor & Contractor Management:


	Identify, engage, supervise, and evaluate technical vendors, contractors, cleaners, security, and service providers.
	Negotiate contracts, monitor SLAs, and ensure strict compliance with quality, safety, and performance standards.


Client & Stakeholder Management:


	Serve as the primary point of contact for all facility-related matters.
	Manage user complaints professionally while maintaining excellent relationships.
	Ensure high levels of client satisfaction through proactive communication and timely issue resolution.


Reporting, Compliance & Finance:


	Prepare detailed monthly technical and operational reports on asset performance, maintenance activities, power consumption, and expenses.
	Maintain accurate records of maintenance history, warranties, service contracts, and regulatory compliance.
	Manage facility budgets, control costs, and identify opportunities for operational efficiency.


Requirements & Qualifications


	10 - 15 years of post-qualification experience in Facility Management, with at least 5 years in a senior technical role.
	Bachelor’s degree inElectrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Estate Management, or Facilities Management. (A Master’s degree or relevant professional certifications).
	Strong hands-on experiencein Power Management — Generators (Diesel & Gas), Solar Systems, EKEDC coordination, and Air Conditioning systems ismandatory.
	Proven track record in building maintenance, painting schedules, civil repairs, and facility refurbishment.
	Excellent vendor management, negotiation, and client relationship skills.
	Strong knowledge of Health, Safety, and Environmental regulations.


Key Competencies:


	Exceptional problem-solving and decision-making skills under pressure.
	Strong leadership and team management ability.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Highly organized with strong attention to detail.
	Ability to respond swiftly and effectively to emergencies.


What We Offer


	Competitive remuneration package (?350,000 – ?450,000 gross per month)
	Comprehensive Health Insurance (HMO)
	Pension Scheme
	Professional development and certification support.


Method of Application 
Interested andsuitably qualified candidatesshould send theirdetailed CV and abrief Cover Letter to: [email protected] using "Senior Facility Manager – Anthony, Lagos Mainland" as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only candidates with a minimum of 10 years of relevant experience will be considered.
	Applications without the correct email subject will not be treated.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Video Editor (Digital Learning Content)",
  "description": "UNICCON Group is a leading African tech conglomerate revolutionizing industries through cutting-edge AI, robotics, drone technology, and smart enterprise solutions. We are dedicated to shaping the future of technology, delivering value to businesses, governments, and individuals across the continent and beyond.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Video Editor (Digital Learning Content)

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type:

Job Purpose


	The Video Editor will be responsible for assembling high-quality instructional videos for the Learning Management System (LMS). This role requires speed, consistency, and attention to detail to meet structured production targets while maintaining engaging and pedagogically sound content.


Key Responsibilities


	Edit and assemble raw footage into polished instructional videos aligned with LMS standards
	Ensure consistency in visual style, pacing, transitions, and audio quality across all courses
	Incorporate graphics, subtitles, animations, and branding elements into video content
	Synchronize audio, visuals, and on-screen text for optimal learner comprehension
	Optimize video formats for web-based LMS platforms (compression, resolution, file size)
	Collaborate with Motion Graphics Designers and Graphic Designers to integrate assets
	Follow course templates and production workflows to ensure uniformity
	Manage multiple video projects simultaneously within tight deadlines
	Conduct quality checks and revisions based on feedback


Performance Expectations (KPIs)


	Deliver 1 completed video every 4 working days
	Achieve minimum of 5 high-quality videos per month
	Maintain low revision rates (&le; 2 rounds per video)
	Adhere to production timelines contributing to 25 videos/month team output


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or recognised certifications in Film Production, Media, Communications, or related field
	3&ndash;5 years of professional video editing experience
	Proven experience editing educational or e-learning content is highly preferred


Technical Skills:


	Proficiency in tools such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, After Effects
	Strong understanding of video formats, codecs, and compression
	Experience with audio editing and color correction
	Familiarity with LMS video requirements is an advantage


Core Competencies:


	Speed with precision
	Attention to detail
	Time management and deadline discipline
	Collaboration and communication
	Adaptability in high-volume production environments.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Intrested and qualified candidates should&nbsp;should be send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@uniccongroup.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Video Editor (Digital Learning Content) Job at Uniccon Group




        
    

        
        UNICCON Group is a leading African tech conglomerate revolutionizing industries through cutting-edge AI, robotics, drone technology, and smart enterprise solutions. We are dedicated to shaping the future of technology, delivering value to businesses, governments, and individuals across the continent and beyond.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Video Editor (Digital Learning Content)

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type:

Job Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Video Editor will be responsible for assembling high-quality instructional videos for the Learning Management System (LMS). This role requires speed, consistency, and attention to detail to meet structured production targets while maintaining engaging and pedagogically sound content.


Key Responsibilities


	Edit and assemble raw footage into polished instructional videos aligned with LMS standards
	Ensure consistency in visual style, pacing, transitions, and audio quality across all courses
	Incorporate graphics, subtitles, animations, and branding elements into video content
	Synchronize audio, visuals, and on-screen text for optimal learner comprehension
	Optimize video formats for web-based LMS platforms (compression, resolution, file size)
	Collaborate with Motion Graphics Designers and Graphic Designers to integrate assets
	Follow course templates and production workflows to ensure uniformity
	Manage multiple video projects simultaneously within tight deadlines
	Conduct quality checks and revisions based on feedback


Performance Expectations (KPIs)


	Deliver 1 completed video every 4 working days
	Achieve minimum of 5 high-quality videos per month
	Maintain low revision rates (≤ 2 rounds per video)
	Adhere to production timelines contributing to 25 videos/month team output


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s degree or recognised certifications in Film Production, Media, Communications, or related field
	3–5 years of professional video editing experience
	Proven experience editing educational or e-learning content is highly preferred


Technical Skills:


	Proficiency in tools such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, After Effects
	Strong understanding of video formats, codecs, and compression
	Experience with audio editing and color correction
	Familiarity with LMS video requirements is an advantage


Core Competencies:


	Speed with precision
	Attention to detail
	Time management and deadline discipline
	Collaboration and communication
